Title: xProfile - currentCustomerAddresses Happy Path Returns Addresses and term_facets
Preconditions: BROWSER-ONLY: Execute in GraphiQL UI ({{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql) via playwright browser. Use CodeMirror editor per graphiql-interaction.md. Do NOT use curl. User authenticated via Headers tab. User has at least 1 saved address.
Steps:
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token grant_type=password&scope=offline_access&username={{USER_EMAIL}}&password={{USER_PASSWORD}}&storeId={{STORE_ID}} → save bearer token
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Click 'Headers' button → set auth token via execCommand('insertText') on Headers textarea → screenshot to verify token in Headers panel
[ACT] Click query editor → Ctrl+A → Backspace to clear
[ACT] Type query: query { currentCustomerAddresses(first: 20) { totalCount items { id city countryCode countryName firstName lastName line1 line2 postalCode regionId regionName phone email addressType isDefault isFavorite description } term_facets { name terms { term label count } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — inspect structure
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[ERRORS] errors[] is empty
[DATA] totalCount is a non-negative integer
[DATA] items[] is an array (may be empty for new accounts; non-empty for seeded accounts)
[DATA] each item has id, city, countryCode, line1 as non-null strings
[DATA] term_facets is an array (may be empty if no addresses)
[DATA] each term_facets entry has name (string) and terms (array)
[DATA] each terms entry has term, label, count fields
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute same query → same totalCount returned
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; currentCustomerAddresses field not found (schema mismatch); term_facets is null instead of empty array; item fields return null for non-null fields

Title: xProfile - currentCustomerAddresses Keyword Filtering
Preconditions: BROWSER-ONLY: GraphiQL with auth. User has 3+ saved addresses including one with a distinct city 'Portland'. Per BA report: keyword matches name/line1/city/countryName/regionName/postalCode.
Steps:
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token → save bearer token
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Set auth token in Headers tab
[ACT] Click query editor → Ctrl+A → Backspace
[ACT] Type query: query { currentCustomerAddresses(keyword: "Portland") { totalCount items { id city line1 } term_facets { name terms { term label count } } } }
[GQL] Execute (▶)
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — inspect filtered results
[ACT] Clear editor → type query with uppercase variant: query { currentCustomerAddresses(keyword: "PORTLAND") { totalCount items { id city } } }
[GQL] Execute (▶)
[READ] Read response — verify case-insensitive match
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[ERRORS] errors[] is empty for both queries
[DATA] keyword='Portland' returns only addresses where city or other searchable fields contain 'Portland'
[DATA] keyword='PORTLAND' returns same totalCount as keyword='Portland' (case-insensitive)
[COUNT] totalCount reduced compared to unfiltered query when keyword is specific
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Clear keyword → re-query without keyword → original count restored
[CONSOLE] No JS errors
Failure_Signals: keyword arg rejected as unknown (DV-008); case-sensitive filtering (different counts for Portland vs PORTLAND); errors[] non-empty

Title: xProfile - currentCustomerAddresses Facet Arguments (countryCodes / regionIds / cities)
Preconditions: BROWSER-ONLY: GraphiQL with auth. User/org has addresses across multiple countries/states/cities (SEED required per OQ-5).
Steps:
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token grant_type=password&scope=offline_access&username={{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}&password={{ORG_USER_PASSWORD}}&storeId={{STORE_ID}} → save bearer token
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Set auth token in Headers tab
[ACT] Click query editor → Ctrl+A → Backspace
[ACT] Type query: query { currentCustomerAddresses(first: 20 countryCodes: "US") { totalCount items { id countryCode } term_facets { name terms { term label count } } } }
[GQL] Execute (▶)
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Verify only US addresses returned
[ACT] Clear editor → type combined query: query { currentCustomerAddresses(first: 20 countryCodes: "US" cities: "New York") { totalCount items { id city countryCode } } }
[GQL] Execute (▶)
[READ] Verify US + New York filter applied
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data
[ERRORS] errors[] is empty for all queries
[DATA] countryCodes='US' filter returns only items where countryCode = 'US'
[DATA] combined countryCodes + cities filter returns subset of single-country result
[COUNT] combined filter totalCount <= single-country filter totalCount
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No JS errors
[ROUNDTRIP] Query without any facets → totalCount equals sum of all facet-filtered subsets
Failure_Signals: countryCodes/cities args not found by schema (DV-008); filter not applied (same results as unfiltered); errors[] non-empty with 'argument not found'

Title: xProfile - currentCustomerAddresses Unauthenticated Returns 401
Preconditions: BROWSER-ONLY: GraphiQL without auth token set. No auth header.
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Ensure NO auth token is set in Headers tab (clear if present)
[ACT] Click query editor → Ctrl+A → Backspace
[ACT] Type query: query { currentCustomerAddresses(first: 5) { totalCount items { id } } }
[GQL] Execute (▶)
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — verify auth error
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows error or 401 condition
[ERRORS] errors[] is non-empty OR HTTP response is 401/403
[DATA] No address items returned
[DATA] Error message references authorization/authentication (not a server error)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No server errors (401 is expected, 500 is not)
Failure_Signals: 200 OK with address data returned without auth (security defect); 500 error instead of 401/403

Title: xProfile - currentOrganizationAddresses Happy Path Returns Org Addresses
Preconditions: BROWSER-ONLY: GraphiQL with org user auth. User is org member. SEED REQUIRED (OQ-5): org has 3+ addresses.
Steps:
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token grant_type=password&scope=offline_access&username={{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}&password={{ORG_USER_PASSWORD}}&storeId={{STORE_ID}} → save bearer token
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Set auth token in Headers tab
[ACT] Click query editor → Ctrl+A → Backspace
[ACT] Type query: query { currentOrganizationAddresses(first: 20) { totalCount items { id city countryCode countryName firstName lastName line1 postalCode regionName isDefault isFavorite } term_facets { name terms { term label count } } } }
[GQL] Execute (▶)
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — verify org address scope
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[ERRORS] errors[] is empty
[DATA] totalCount is a non-negative integer
[DATA] items[] contains org-scoped addresses (distinct from personal addresses)
[DATA] term_facets populated with at least country facet if addresses span countries
[DATA] each item has id, line1, city, countryCode as strings
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Query currentCustomerAddresses with same token → compare; org addresses should only appear in currentOrganizationAddresses
[CONSOLE] No JS errors
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; currentOrganizationAddresses field not found (schema mismatch); org addresses appear in personal query (scope leak); isFavorite-first sort not applied
References: Source: VCST-4710 / ba-report-2026-04-23; graphql-schema.md — currentOrganizationAddresses; BL-B2B-001

Steps:
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token grant_type=password&scope=offline_access&username={{USER_EMAIL}}&password={{USER_PASSWORD}}&storeId={{STORE_ID}} → save bearer token for personal account (non-org user)
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Set auth token in Headers tab
[ACT] Click query editor → Ctrl+A → Backspace
[ACT] Type query: query { currentOrganizationAddresses(first: 5) { totalCount items { id } } }
[GQL] Execute (▶)
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — verify 403 or empty data with auth error
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data or error
[ERRORS] errors[] is non-empty with authorization error OR totalCount = 0 with no items (scoped to org = empty for non-org user)
[DATA] No org addresses returned for a non-org user

Title: xProfile - checkDuplicateAddress Returns isDuplicated True for Existing Address
Preconditions: BROWSER-ONLY: GraphiQL with auth. User has a known saved address. OQ-8 must be resolved.
Test_Data: storeId={{STORE_ID}}; user_email={{USER_EMAIL}}; user_password={{USER_PASSWORD}}; known_member_id=<resolved at runtime from me { contact { id } }>
Steps:
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token → save bearer token
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Set auth token in Headers tab
[ACT] Click query editor → Ctrl+A → Backspace
[ACT] Type query to get memberId: query { me { contact { id } } }
[GQL] Execute (▶)
[VAR] Save contact.id as <MEMBER_ID>
[ACT] Clear editor → type duplicate check query using known address fields: query { checkDuplicateAddress(memberId: "<MEMBER_ID>" address: { line1: "<existing-line1>" city: "<existing-city>" countryCode: "<existing-countryCode>" }) { isDuplicated } }
[GQL] Execute (▶)
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[ERRORS] errors[] is empty
[DATA] isDuplicated = true for an address that matches an existing saved address
[DATA] isDuplicated is a boolean (not null)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Query currentCustomerAddresses → confirm the address used in check exists in items[]
[CONSOLE] No JS errors
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty for valid input; isDuplicated = false for a genuine duplicate (false negative); isDuplicated is null instead of boolean; checkDuplicateAddress field not found
References: Source: VCST-4710 / ba-report-2026-04-23; graphql-schema.md — checkDuplicateAddress; AC-13

Title: xProfile - checkDuplicateAddress Returns isDuplicated False for New Address

Steps:
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token → save bearer token
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Set auth token in Headers tab
[ACT] Click query editor → Ctrl+A → Backspace
[ACT] Type query to get memberId: query { me { contact { id } } }
[GQL] Execute (▶)
[VAR] Save contact.id as <MEMBER_ID>
[ACT] Clear editor → type duplicate check for a non-existent address: query { checkDuplicateAddress(memberId: "<MEMBER_ID>" address: { line1: "9999 Unique St Never Exists" city: "NoSuchCity" countryCode: "US" }) { isDuplicated } }
[GQL] Execute (▶)
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[ERRORS] errors[] is empty
[DATA] isDuplicated = false for an address that does not exist in user's address book
[DATA] isDuplicated is a boolean
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty for valid novel address; isDuplicated = true for non-existent address (false positive); field not found
References: Source: VCST-4710 / ba-report-2026-04-23; graphql-schema.md — checkDuplicateAddress; AC-13
